substances.name: Lidocaine
toxicities: Systemic lidocaine toxicity can include neurologic symptoms, seizures, hypotension, conduction abnormalities, arrhythmias, and cardiovascular collapse.
Toxicity risk depends on concentration, infusion timing, hepatic function, acid-base status, and clinical context.
substances.summary: 利多卡因是局部麻醉药和 Ib 类抗心律失常药；本条目区分药代时间进程、TDM 资料和临床处置。 Lidocaine is a local anesthetic and Class Ib antiarrhythmic. This entry separates pharmacokinetic/TDM reference data from clinical treatment decisions and is for educational indexing only.

majorWarnings: High systemic concentrations can be associated with neurologic and cardiovascular toxicity.
majorInteractions: Other sodium channel blockers or antiarrhythmics can have additive cardiac or neurologic toxicity.
Propranolol and other interacting drugs may reduce lidocaine clearance or increase toxicity risk.
monitoring: Serum lidocaine concentration when clinically indicated

tdm.monitoringType: serum or plasma concentration when clinically indicated
tdm.samplingTime: Interpret relative to infusion timing, dose changes, organ function, and symptoms.

therapeuticRanges.note: Laboratory reference interval; not a prescribing target.

toxicityThresholds.note: DailyMed and laboratory references associate increasing adverse manifestations with concentrations above this range.

criticalValues.note: Reported as a critical value by Mayo-style test catalog.

interpretationCaveats: Concentration interpretation must be tied to sampling time and clinical status.
Toxicity assessment also depends on ECG, neurologic symptoms, acid-base status, hepatic function, and interacting drugs.

substances.name: Procainamide
toxicities: Procainamide and NAPA toxicity can include QRS widening, QT prolongation, hypotension, ventricular arrhythmias, torsades de pointes, and lupus-like syndrome.
Renal impairment can increase NAPA accumulation and changes interpretation.
substances.summary: 普鲁卡因胺是 Ia 类抗心律失常药；资料索引时需注意其活性代谢物 NAPA 与浓度监测解释。 Procainamide is a Class Ia antiarrhythmic. TDM entries should account for both procainamide and its active metabolite NAPA.

majorWarnings: Concentration interpretation must be combined with ECG intervals, renal function, symptoms, and co-medications.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging antiarrhythmics can increase proarrhythmia risk.
Renal function and drugs affecting conduction or repolarization materially affect interpretation.
monitoring: Procainamide and NAPA concentrations when clinically indicated

sourceRefs.title: Mayo Clinic Laboratories procainamide and NAPA serum

tdm.monitoringType: serum concentration of procainamide and NAPA
tdm.samplingTime: Use the laboratory catalog and clinical protocol for peak/trough timing; source-specific timing should be verified before clinical use.

criticalValues.note: Procainamide critical value in Mayo catalog.
NAPA critical value in Mayo catalog.

interpretationCaveats: Procainamide and NAPA must be interpreted together.
Renal function and timing relative to dosing strongly affect interpretation.
ECG findings and symptoms are essential; concentration alone is not a treatment algorithm.

sourceRefs.title: Mayo Clinic Laboratories procainamide and NAPA serum

substances.name: Flecainide
toxicities: Flecainide toxicity can include QRS widening, PR prolongation, bradycardia, ventricular arrhythmias, heart failure worsening, dizziness, visual disturbance, and cardiac arrest in severe overdose.
Adverse cardiac events increase when trough concentrations exceed the usual reference range.
substances.summary: 氟卡尼是 Ic 类抗心律失常药；部分场景会结合血药浓度、心电图 QRS 改变和相互作用进行解释。 Flecainide is a Class Ic antiarrhythmic with concentration and ECG monitoring relevance in selected patients.

majorWarnings: Proarrhythmia and QRS widening risk require clinical and ECG context.
majorInteractions: Amiodarone and CYP2D6-related interactions may increase exposure.
monitoring: Trough flecainide concentration in selected situations

tdm.monitoringType: trough plasma concentration in selected patients
tdm.samplingTime: Trough concentration is typically referenced; verify timing with the local laboratory.

therapeuticRanges.note: DailyMed and laboratory catalogs describe this trough range.

toxicityThresholds.note: Higher trough concentrations are associated with increased adverse cardiac experiences.

interpretationCaveats: DailyMed specifically emphasizes monitoring in severe renal failure, severe hepatic disease, and with concurrent amiodarone.
Interpret with ECG, QRS duration, renal/hepatic function, dose timing, and symptoms.

dangerous: Structural heart disease contexts where contraindicated by label

substances.name: Amiodarone
toxicities: Amiodarone can cause pulmonary toxicity, hepatic injury, thyroid dysfunction, ocular toxicity, neurologic toxicity, bradycardia, QT prolongation, and multiple organ-system adverse effects.
Because of long tissue persistence and active metabolites, adverse effects and interactions may persist after discontinuation.
substances.summary: 胺碘酮是 III 类抗心律失常药；血药浓度解释复杂，不能作为单独的给药调整依据。 Amiodarone is a Class III antiarrhythmic. Plasma concentration interpretation is complex and must not be treated as standalone dose guidance.

majorWarnings: Pulmonary, hepatic, thyroid, ocular, neurologic, and cardiac toxicities require clinical monitoring.
majorInteractions: Amiodarone has many clinically important drug interactions.

notes: Amiodarone pharmacokinetics are complex because of tissue accumulation and active metabolites.
Do not infer clinical response or dose adjustment from Tmax alone.

tdm.monitoringType: serum or plasma concentration in selected patients
tdm.samplingTime: Trough sampling is commonly referenced by laboratory catalogs; verify local requirements.

therapeuticRanges.note: Mayo reference interval for trough concentration.

toxicityThresholds.note: Mayo catalog toxic concentration threshold for amiodarone.

interpretationCaveats: Desethylamiodarone has similar activity, but the cited catalog does not define a separate therapeutic range.
Clinical toxicity monitoring remains central; concentration does not replace ECG, thyroid, hepatic, pulmonary, ocular, or symptom monitoring.

sourceRefs.title: Mayo Clinic Laboratories amiodarone serum/plasma catalog PDF

dangerous: Severe bradycardia or heart block without pacing

substances.name: Quinidine
toxicities: Quinidine can cause QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, conduction disturbances, hypotension, cinchonism, thrombocytopenia, and other serious adverse effects.
Concentration interpretation must be combined with ECG, QRS/QT, symptoms, potassium/magnesium, renal/hepatic function, and interacting drugs.
substances.summary: 奎尼丁是 Ia 类抗心律失常药，具有 TDM 相关性；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Quinidine is a Class Ia antiarrhythmic with TDM relevance. This entry is educational reference only.

majorWarnings: Can prolong QT interval and cause torsades de pointes.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging drugs can increase proarrhythmia risk.
CYP and P-gp interactions may be clinically important.

notes: source needed: add label-verified Tmax, half-life, and formulation-specific duration before displaying numeric values.
TDM peak/trough sampling times differ by quinidine salt/formulation.

tdm.samplingTime: Peak and trough timing depends on salt/formulation; Mayo catalog describes quinidine sulfate peak about 1.5 hours after dose and trough immediately before the next dose.
interpretationCaveats: Interpret with sampling time, ECG, electrolytes, symptoms, organ function, formulation, and interacting drugs.
Therapeutic ranges are not stand-alone treatment instructions.

sourceRefs.title: Mayo Clinic Laboratories quinidine serum PDF

substances.name: Disopyramide
toxicities: Disopyramide can cause QT prolongation, ventricular arrhythmias, conduction slowing, negative inotropy, heart failure worsening, and anticholinergic adverse effects.
Use with other negative inotropes or QT-prolonging drugs requires clinical caution.
substances.summary: 丙吡胺是 Ia 类抗心律失常药；本条目用于药代、毒性和相互作用资料索引。 Disopyramide is a Class Ia antiarrhythmic. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Life-threatening ventricular arrhythmia contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Can worsen heart failure because of negative inotropic effect.
QT prolongation and proarrhythmia risk require ECG context.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging antiarrhythmics and beta blockers can increase cardiac risk.
Anticholinergic burden can be clinically relevant.

notes: source needed: add label-verified Tmax, half-life, and duration for immediate-release and controlled-release formulations separately.

tdm.monitoringType: serum concentration may be used in selected patients; not encoded as a routine range in this pass
tdm.reason: This pass records the structure and source link but avoids encoding a therapeutic range without a verified laboratory catalog source.
interpretationCaveats: source needed: add a verified laboratory TDM catalog before displaying therapeutic or toxic ranges.
Interpret with ECG, QRS/QT, heart failure symptoms, anticholinergic effects, renal function, and interacting drugs.

substances.name: Mexiletine
toxicities: Mexiletine can cause neurologic, gastrointestinal, and cardiac adverse effects; toxic concentrations may be associated with hypotension, tremor, and cardiac abnormalities.
Overdose management is supportive with close monitoring of vital signs and rhythm.
substances.summary: 美西律是口服 Ib 类抗心律失常药；本条目用于学习其时间进程、毒性和相互作用资料。 Mexiletine is an oral Class Ib antiarrhythmic related electrophysiologically to lidocaine. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Ventricular arrhythmia contexts depending on label
majorWarnings: Cardiac and neurologic toxicity assessment requires clinical and ECG context.
majorInteractions: CYP1A2/CYP2D6-related interactions may alter exposure.
Other antiarrhythmics can have additive electrophysiologic effects.
monitoring: Serum mexiletine concentration in selected patients

notes: source needed: add label-verified Tmax and half-life for the selected formulation before displaying numeric time-course values.

tdm.monitoringType: serum or plasma concentration in selected patients
tdm.samplingTime: Trough concentrations are most reproducible and should be collected immediately before the next scheduled dose at steady state.

therapeuticRanges.note: Labcorp and other laboratory catalogs use this trough reference range.

interpretationCaveats: Interpret with ECG, neurologic symptoms, hepatic function, dose timing, and interacting drugs.
Toxicity can occur outside simple concentration thresholds depending on patient context.

sourceRefs.title: ARUP Laboratories mexiletine serum or plasma

substances.name: Propafenone
toxicities: Propafenone can cause proarrhythmia, conduction slowing, bradycardia, hypotension, heart failure worsening, seizures, and high-grade ventricular arrhythmias in overdose.
It has beta-blocking properties that may add to other negative chronotropic or inotropic drugs.
substances.summary: 普罗帕酮是 Ic 类抗心律失常药，并具有一定 β 阻滞特性；本条目仅用于学习参考。 Propafenone is a Class Ic antiarrhythmic with sodium-channel blocking and beta-blocking properties. This entry is educational reference only.

contraindications: Bronchospastic disorders or severe obstructive pulmonary disease
majorWarnings: Proarrhythmia and conduction slowing require ECG context.
CYP2D6 and CYP3A4 interactions can increase exposure and adverse reaction risk.
majorInteractions: CYP2D6 inhibitors and CYP3A4 inhibitors can increase exposure.
Propafenone can increase digoxin or warfarin levels.
Lidocaine may increase CNS adverse effects.
monitoring: Digoxin or warfarin monitoring if coadministered

tmax.note: Peak plasma levels occur approximately 3.5 hours after administration in most individuals.

notes: Propafenone has nonlinear pharmacokinetics and substantial interindividual variability.
Do not infer clinical effect or safety from Tmax alone.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Clinical use is generally monitored by ECG, symptoms, contraindications, organ function, and interacting drugs rather than plasma concentration.
interpretationCaveats: CYP2D6 phenotype and CYP3A4/CYP2D6 inhibitors can materially change exposure.

substances.name: Sotalol
toxicities: Sotalol can cause QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, bradycardia, hypotension, bronchospasm, heart failure, and hypoglycemia.
Overdose has resulted in death and may involve QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, ventricular tachycardia, bradycardia, asystole, and hypotension.
substances.summary: 索他洛尔兼具 II 类 β 阻滞和 III 类抗心律失常作用；资料解释时需重视 QT 间期和肾功能背景。 Sotalol has both beta-blocking Class II and Class III antiarrhythmic properties. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Atrial fibrillation/flutter and ventricular arrhythmia contexts depending on label
contraindications: Sinus bradycardia or AV block without pacemaker
Congenital or acquired long QT syndrome
Cardiogenic shock or decompensated heart failure
Baseline QT interval contraindications for AF/AFL labels
majorWarnings: QT prolongation and torsades de pointes risk require ECG and renal function context.
Renal elimination makes dosing interval and accumulation dependent on kidney function.
majorInteractions: Avoid Class I or Class III antiarrhythmics and other QT-prolonging drugs.
Digoxin, diltiazem, verapamil, and beta blockers increase bradycardia/hypotension/heart failure risk.
Aluminum- or magnesium-containing antacids can reduce exposure.

notes: QT effect and torsades risk depend on plasma concentration, renal function, electrolytes, dose timing, and interacting drugs.
Steady-state is reached after about 5 to 6 doses when administered twice daily.

tdm.monitoringType: serum concentration available in selected contexts, but routine management focuses on ECG/QT and renal function

therapeuticRanges.note: Mayo/Labcorp reference; toxic range has not been established.

interpretationCaveats: This is not a substitute for QT monitoring and renal-function-based clinical decisions.
Toxic range has not been established in the cited laboratory catalog.

dangerous: Congenital or acquired long QT syndrome

substances.name: Dofetilide
toxicities: Dofetilide can cause serious ventricular arrhythmias, primarily torsades de pointes associated with QT prolongation.
QT prolongation is directly related to plasma concentration; renal impairment and interacting drugs increase risk.
substances.summary: 多非利特是 III 类 IKr 阻滞剂；资料索引时应强调 QT 风险和肾功能相关信息。 Dofetilide is a Class III antiarrhythmic IKr blocker. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Atrial fibrillation/flutter rhythm-control contexts depending on label
contraindications: Congenital or acquired long QT syndrome
Severe renal impairment below label creatinine-clearance threshold
Verapamil, cimetidine, trimethoprim, ketoconazole, hydrochlorothiazide, and other listed interacting drugs
majorWarnings: Initiation requires ECG monitoring in an appropriate facility according to labeling.
Creatinine clearance and QT monitoring are central because concentration relates to QT prolongation.
majorInteractions: Renal cation transport inhibitors and hydrochlorothiazide can substantially increase risk.
Other QT-prolonging drugs can increase proarrhythmia risk.

notes: Dofetilide QT effect is concentration related.
Clinical use requires label-specific ECG and renal function procedures; this entry is not dosing guidance.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration; ECG/QT and renal function are central
tdm.reason: Although plasma concentration relates to QT prolongation, clinical labeling uses creatinine clearance and ECG/QT monitoring rather than routine plasma concentration TDM.
interpretationCaveats: This entry must not be used to initiate, prescribe, or adjust dofetilide.

substances.name: Ibutilide
toxicities: Ibutilide can induce or worsen ventricular arrhythmias, including torsades de pointes and polymorphic ventricular tachycardia.
Risk is worsened by QT prolongation, bradycardia, hypokalemia, and other patient factors.
substances.summary: 伊布利特是静脉用 III 类抗心律失常药；本条目仅用于学习参考和数据索引。 Ibutilide is an IV Class III antiarrhythmic. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Atrial fibrillation/flutter conversion contexts depending on label
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to ibutilide or product components
majorWarnings: Proarrhythmia may be potentially fatal and requires monitored administration.
Hypokalemia and baseline QT prolongation increase concern.
majorInteractions: Other QT-prolonging drugs can increase torsades risk.
Digoxin toxicity may be masked by supraventricular arrhythmias.
monitoring: Readiness to manage torsades de pointes

notes: source needed: add label-verified effect onset, offset, and half-life before displaying numeric time-course values.
Continuous ECG monitoring is more important than a simple duration estimate.

tdm.monitoringType: not routinely monitored by plasma concentration
tdm.reason: Monitoring focuses on continuous ECG, QT, electrolytes, and immediate arrhythmia management rather than plasma concentration.

substances.name: Adenosine
toxicities: Adenosine can cause transient AV block, bradycardia, flushing, chest discomfort, dyspnea, bronchospasm, hypotension, and rare serious arrhythmias.
Effects are very short-lived because adenosine is rapidly cleared from blood.
substances.summary: 腺苷是超短效静脉用 AV 结传导阻滞相关药物；本条目用于时间进程和相互作用资料索引。 Adenosine is an ultra-short-acting IV AV nodal blocker used in selected supraventricular tachycardia contexts. This entry is educational reference only.

indications: Paroxysmal supraventricular tachycardia contexts depending on label
contraindications: Second- or third-degree AV block without pacemaker
Known or suspected bronchoconstrictive or bronchospastic lung disease in some labels
majorWarnings: Can cause transient or prolonged AV block, bradycardia, and arrhythmias.
Bronchospasm can occur in susceptible patients.
majorInteractions: Dipyridamole can potentiate adenosine effect.
Methylxanthines such as caffeine or theophylline can antagonize adenosine effect.
Carbamazepine may increase heart block risk.
monitoring: Readiness for resuscitation in monitored clinical setting

onset.note: Very rapid onset after IV bolus; exact timing is administration-dependent.

durationOfAction.note: Effects are generally very brief because adenosine is rapidly taken up and metabolized.

eliminationHalfLife.note: Often described as less than 10 seconds.

notes: Adenosine timing is highly route/administration dependent and should not be generalized to oral supplements.
Continuous ECG and clinical monitoring are central.

tdm.reason: Adenosine is ultra-short acting and monitored by immediate ECG/clinical response rather than plasma concentration.

dangerous: Second- or third-degree AV block without pacemaker
